page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded operations. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2153.0 and a multi-core score of 17709.0, both placing in the strong tier. It demonstrates high efficiency in text processing, scoring 2873.0 in Geekbench text processing (top tier), and shows strong capabilities in cryptographic tasks with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 36151.65. For memory-bound workloads, the DDR5 memory architecture delivers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2557.11 GB/sec.

The c8i-flex.8xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require high-speed processing but do not demand local NVMe storage. It is highly suitable for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and small-to-medium language model inference, where it achieves 377.41 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. As a flex instance, it offers a cost-effective profile for dedicated compute resources, balancing high-frequency Intel Xeon cores with a moderate 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers strong benchmark performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2153.0 and a multi-core score of 17709.0. It excels in text processing, cryptographic operations, and small-scale LLM inference, achieving 377.41 tokens/sec for 135M model text generation. With a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, this server is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and data processing that require high-frequency CPU performance without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU at 3.9 GHz, delivering strong performance for compilation and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores at 3.9 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Strong performance in text processing, software compilation, and cryptography
Baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps without local storage overhead
Well-suited for static web serving, software development, and small LLM inference
